body:
## OpenCode Review — [TASK-07] PvP Kill Feed

**Decision: APPROVED — merging.**

### Checklist

| Check | Result |
|---|---|
| `KILL_FEED` added to `shared/packets.ts` | ✅ ID 23, no existing IDs renumbered |
| Packet ID 23 is free in the protocol table | ✅ (21=PONG, 22=RESPAWN not yet in shared enum, 23 is clean) |
| Server emits on PvP kill only | ✅ Inside `case EntityType.PLAYER` block, `p.hp <= 0` guard |
| Server broadcasts to all clients | ✅ `io.emit` (global broadcast — correct for a kill feed) |
| Packet layout consistent server→client | ✅ `[23, killerId, killerNick, victimId, victimNick]` — `data[2]`/`data[4]` indices match |
| Client handles packet and renders UI | ✅ `KillFeed.ts` — DOM overlay, top-center, last 5 kills, 5s fade |
| No new npm dependencies | ✅ |
| Branch name | ✅ `feat/task-07-kill-feed` |
| PR title format | ✅ `[TASK-07] add PvP kill feed` |
| Build (self-reported) | ✅ passes |

### Notes

- `KillFeedEvent` interface is well-structured and the `kills` return value cleanly extends `processAttack` without breaking existing callers.
- **Minor edge case (non-blocking):** The kill push uses `if (p.hp <= 0)` after applying damage, with no guard on whether the player was already at 0 HP before this hit. Rapid multi-attacker scenarios could produce a duplicate kill event. A follow-up fix should gate the push on a `>0 → ≤0` transition (`const wasAlive = p.hp > 0; /* apply damage */; if (wasAlive && p.hp <= 0) kills.push(...)`). Tracking as a known issue.
- `RESPAWN` (ID 22) is referenced in the client constants but missing from `shared/packets.ts` — pre-existing gap not introduced by this PR; should be cleaned up in a separate task.

All required functionality is present and correct. Merging.
